paper.title_en: Effects of generalized entropy on Kerr-Newman black hole thermodynamics
paper.abstract_en: In this article, we investigate the thermodynamic quantities of Kerr-Newmann black hole (KNBH) within the framework of three parameters (generalized) and exponential entropies. It is observed that black hole mass (positive increasing behavior) and temperature (remains positive) provide physically acceptable and appealing results for both entropies. In these frameworks, we develop various thermodynamic relations such as heat capacity, Hessian matrix, energy emission, sparsity of Hawking radiations, microcanonical ensembles and geometrothermodynmics (through Wienhold and Ruppeiner geometries). The analysis of heat capacity reveals the existence of phase transition points separating stable and unstable regions. The Hessian matrix formalism confirms local stability through appropriate positive condition in valid parameter spaces. The behavior of energy emission and sparsity parameters demonstrate that generalized and exponential entropy formalism significantly influence the Hawking radiations. The microcanonical ensemble analysis suggests that the black hole remains locally stable under a wide range of parameters. In the context of geometrothermodynamics, scalar curvature remains positive in most cases which indicate stable configurations and the presence of repulsive interactions. Our results demonstrate that modifications of entropies play a fundamental role in elucidate the intricate phase structure and stability of black hole.
